subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and focused research question directly linked to each section
Appropriate selection of secondary sources (court documents, marketâshare data, academic journals)
Introduction effectively frames the investigationâs significance within the Google antitrust context
weaknesses: Methodology lacks critical details on search strategy and inclusion/exclusion criteria
Occasional digressions in context narrative reduce the tightness of focus
Thesis statement is verbose and introduces slight ambiguity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate consistently communicates the topic and research question with disciplineâappropriate clarity and purpose, meriting performance in the 3â4 band. Evidence of informed source selection supports the investigation, but gaps in procedural transparency (no search strategy or reliability checks) keep this from reaching the top band. Overall, a mark of 4 judiciously reflects strong focus and method with room for more methodological rigor.

strengths: Accurate and coherent use of core economic concepts (network effects, residual demand)
Consistent and mostly correct application of terminology adapted to digital-economy contexts
Relevant integration of source material to support theoretical discussion
weaknesses: Source applications occasionally lapse into descriptive narration without deeper linkage to the argument
Bibliographic entries are sometimes incomplete or overly long URLs interrupt flow
Key theoretical definitions lack explicit citations to foundational texts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates clear and coherent disciplinary knowledge and employs appropriate terminology with only minor lapses. Source material is mostly wellâintegrated, but occasional descriptive overuse and citation omissions limit complete mastery. A score of 4 accurately reflects solid knowledge and understanding with identifiable areas for more rigorous source engagement and precision.

strengths: Substantial body of research spanning marketâshare statistics, corporate filings and peerâreviewed studies
Logical, reasoned analysis of market structure variables with supported conclusions
Argument maintains focus on the research question and acknowledges key limitations
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative measures of market concentration (e.g., HHI) to deepen analysis
Critical evaluation of productâdifferentiation strategies remains superficial
Conclusions omit reflection on data limitations and methodological constraints
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidateâs critical thinking is grounded in an extensive and relevant research base and delivers a coherent argument with supported conclusions, placing performance firmly in the midârange of the 4â6 band. However, the absence of quantitative concentration metrics and limited critical evaluation of key assumptions prevent it from attaining higher marks. A mark of 6 fairly rewards substantial analytical effort while signalling the need for deeper evaluative rigour.

strengths: Adheres to the basic EE structure with clear headings and a bibliography
weaknesses: Missing page numbering and presence of code strings/placeholder artefacts
Inconsistent citation formatting and overlong URLs disrupt readability
Layout and formatting errors detract significantly from professionalism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report follows the broad structural conventions, multiple presentation flawsâabsent page numbers, stray code, placeholder tables and irregular formattingâundermine its overall clarity and polish. These pervasive issues place performance at the low end of the band. A score of 1 appropriately reflects minimal adherence to the presentation requirements.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question is explicitly stated, focused and repeatedly referenced throughout the body sections (size/number of firms, entry barriers, product differentiation).  
â¢  Each analytical section maps back to classical structure variables, keeping discussion tightly aligned.  
â¢  The wording of the thesis is somewhat verbose and introduces two different labels (âmix of oligopolistic and monopolistic structuresâ), creating slight ambiguity, but overall clarity and focus remain strong â securely in the top band at a score of 5.

The student presents a precise, discipline-appropriate topic (âWhat is the market structure of the search-engine industry?â) and immediately frames it within the current Google antitrust ruling, demonstrating clear relevance and significance.  
â¢  The introduction specifies why market structure matters (regulatory consequences, network-effects) and outlines how traditional models may not fully fit this sector â signalling a purposeful, well-focused investigation.  
â¢  Minor clarity issues remain â the thesis table at the front contains placeholder numbers and there are occasional digressions when redefining market power â but these do not materially obscure the topic or its appropriateness. Hence performance is solidly within the 5â6 band, closer to 5 than 6.

Source material (StatCounter, Alphabet 10-K, academic papers) is mostly relevant and each citation is integrated into argument (e.g., TAC figures illustrate barriers; bias study supports market-power claim).  
â¢  Application occasionally slips into description (extended explanation of network effects) and some bibliographic details are incomplete or links excessively long, limiting transparency.  
â¢  Overall, application is âmostly relevant and appropriateâ, meriting the 3â4 band, with a score of 4 reflecting its stronger aspects.

The essay follows the required EE structure (abstract removed per new guide, introductionâbodyâconclusion, bibliography). Headings and sub-headings are clear, visual material is integrated, and major elements (content page, bibliography) are present.  
â¢  Placeholder numbering table and the stray code string at the end weaken professionalism but do not remove structural clarity, justifying the upper band score of 3.

The layout is generally neat with consistent fonts and images, yet notable issues persist: missing page numbers, over-long URLs disrupting text flow, uneven table alignment, and a mix of Markdown/HTML artefacts (ââ¦â, code string). â¢ Citation formatting is irregular and some retrieval details are absent. â¢ These faults indicate that several IB presentation conventions are only partially met, placing the work in the 1â2 band at a score of 2.

Discussion weaves arguments on size, barriers and differentiation into a coherent narrative that revisits the research question and recognises limitations (data bias, residual demand).  
â¢  Evaluation is present but not sustained; alternative interpretations (e.g., contestable-market theory) are hinted at but under-developed.  
â¢  The argument is reasoned and partially critical, warranting a 7 within the 7â9 band.

The student moves beyond description: employs region-specific comparisons, integrates cost data with theory, evaluates residual demand, and questions the adequacy of market-share measures.  
â¢  Conclusions follow logically from evidence, though quantification (e.g., HHI) and deeper bias appraisal are absent.  
â¢  Depth therefore aligns with the 7â9 band; an 8 reflects substantial but not fully âeffectiveâ analysis.

The essay displays clear, coherent economic knowledge: definitions of market structures, network effects, residual demand and two-sided markets are accurately explained and used to interpret evidence.  
â¢  Theory is connected to contemporary policy context (DOJ case) and regional idiosyncrasies (Great Firewall), showing good disciplinary understanding.  
â¢  Minor mis-statements (e.g., âmonopoly can set prices below competitive levelsâ) and a few conceptual overlaps prevent a full 6, but command of subject matter is unquestionably effective â 5.

Research base is wide: global/ regional market-share data, corporate financials, peer-reviewed journals, and legal documents, most of which directly address the RQ.  
â¢  Appropriateness is generally clear, yet some sources (blogs, promotional sites) are weaker and citation gaps remain; thus the strand fits comfortably in 7â9, mid-high at 8.

The student explains that only secondary research will be used and lists the types of data (market-share statistics, annual reports, academic journals) plus economic tools (market-share tables, residual-demand concept).  
â¢  Evidence of informed source selection is visibleâcourt documents, StatCounter data and R&D spending figures are appropriate to the question.  
â¢  Nevertheless, the procedure lacks details about search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria and reliability checks; primary data was not considered. These omissions place the work at the upper end of the 3â4 band rather than in 5â6.

Technical termsâoligopoly, economies of scale, network effects, residual demand, two-sided marketâare used accurately and consistently.  
â¢  The student adapts terminology for digital contexts (redefining âpriceâ as attention), demonstrating conceptual flexibility.  
â¢  Minor spelling/typing errors (âmonopolisitcâ) and the placeholder table detract slightly from consistency, placing performance at 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The word count is explicitly stated and falls within the 4,000-word limit, fulfilling the formatting requirement.
The bibliography header lacks hanging indents and consistent formatting. Apply a uniform citation style and indent second lines to improve readability.
Several references omit publication dates or access dates. Ensure each entry includes full bibliographic details (author, date, title, URL, access date).
Stray code appears at the end of the document. Remove any leftover OCR or HTML artifacts to maintain a professional presentation.

The product-differentiation section outlines features but does not critically evaluate how these differences affect market power. Add comparative analysis on how each differentiation strategy shifts demand curves.

The methodology section header is present, but the procedure description lacks detail on search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria and source reliability checks. Expand this to ensure transparency.
The global market-share chart uses data from 2009-2014, which may not reflect current industry dynamics. Update to the latest period to enhance relevance.
The title is precise and discipline-appropriate, clearly framing the investigation around the search-engine market structure and signalling a focused research purpose.
The final reference list is rendered as unstyled HTML list items. Convert to a numbered or bullet list with consistent indentation and remove HTML artifacts.
The table of contents contains placeholder numerals and inconsistent formatting. Update all entries with accurate page numbers and remove placeholders to improve navigation and professionalism.

The context section is descriptive but drifts from the research question. Tighten this narrative by explicitly linking each historical detail back to how it shapes investigation of market structure variables.

The redefinition of market power in the attention economy is innovative, but it lacks a formal citation to foundational two-sided market theory. Incorporate a definitive source to ground this conceptual shift.

The classical definitions of market structures appear lifted directly from IB Economics without attribution. Provide explicit citations for each theoretical definition to demonstrate source engagement.
The assumption excluding AI-based platforms needs stronger justification. Discuss how omitting these emerging players might bias conclusions about overall market structure.
The stated thesis is verbose and combines multiple labels (âoligopolistic and monopolistic structuresâ). Refine to a succinct proposition that directly answers the research question.
The market share discussion names over 30 search engines but lacks quantitative metrics (e.g., HHI) to gauge concentration. Incorporate a concentration ratio to bolster analysis depth.
The brief mention of lower cost-per-click in Bing versus Google invites an elasticity analysis. Quantify how a 70% cost difference alters advertiser demand.
Figure captions should include clear numbering, a concise title, data source and date. Revise the diagram label to comply with IB graphical conventions.
The world-map figure lacks axis labels or a scale and includes an overly detailed alt text block. Simplify the description and ensure legends and scales are clearly marked.
The local-market discussion for Russia and China would benefit from a Herfindahl-Hirschman Index calculation to substantiate claims of duopoly or monopoly.
When discussing regional variations, include the precise date and source of the data within the text. This contextualizes fluctuations in market shares.
A sentence ends abruptly mid-paragraph, indicating an editing oversight. Review and complete the thought to maintain clarity and coherence.
The assertion about search bias lacks any statistical significance testing. Incorporate p-values or confidence intervals to demonstrate the robustness of these comparative findings.
The two-sided market structure concept is introduced but not elaborated with supporting theory or a diagram. Add a formal definition and graphical illustration to reinforce understanding.
Residual demand is discussed qualitatively but without a formal formula (e.g., $$D_r=P igl(Q igl)-	ext{others} igr)$$. Include an equation to clarify the concept.
The DuckDuckGo privacy-focused differentiation claim would be stronger with user-adoption statistics or survey data demonstrating consumer response to this feature.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings but omits reflection on data limitations and methodological weaknesses. Include evaluative commentary on sourcesâ reliability.
